function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'fprintf' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'i < N' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i < N' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i < N' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'i < N' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 12:  <statement> for ( i = 0; i < N ; i++ ) a[i] = i; </statement> is reached, the statement line 23:  <statement> fprintf(stderr, " x = %d\n", x); </statement> has already been reached?
Does the statement line 11:  <statement> float a[N]; </statement> dominate line 20:  <statement> x = a[i] < x ? a[i] : x; </statement>?
data_dependency: Does variable i at line 18: <statement>    for ( i = 0; i < N ; i++ ) </statement> determine the value stored in variable x at line 20: <statement>         x = a[i] < x ? a[i] : x; </statement>?

Does the value of variable i at line 18: <statement>    for ( i = 0; i < N ; i++ ) </statement> depend on the value of variable argc at line 8: <statement> int main (int argc, char **argv) </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'x' live at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'argc' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Is the statement 'fprintf(stderr, " x = %d\n", x);' unreachable?
Can the program actually reach the statement: “int omp_get_num_threads(void);”?
